what_it_does: An IIFE that registers every per-canvas generative move of the app into the CV_AI registry as first-class 'capability' entries, so the catalogue describes the full generative surface (not just the composer's and image's moves). It defines generic run functions (runText/runImgGen/runImgEdit), seeds a TEXT array (~28 text capabilities across color/icon/voice/pattern/component/build/deck/widget/wizard/type/inbox/chat families) and an IMAGE array (2 image moves), plus one bespoke 'deck.titlechain' capability whose prompt is inlined in its run.
observations: Throws on load if window.CV_AI is missing, naming the load-order fix ('load app/ai/ai-registry.js first').
runText completes a.params.prompt (or a.params) — the canvas hands in its own prompt so the prompt text is not duplicated here; the canvas keeps its own parse.
TEXT entries are terse tuples [id, name, family, surfaces[], icon]; all register with behaviours ['voice.conceptv'], provider 'claude', provenance 'built-in', and the generic runText.
IMAGE entries [id,name,family,surfaces,icon,run] register with behaviours [], provider 'openai-image', and the image run functions.
chat.respond has surfaces ['*'] (every surface); most others are surface-scoped.

deck.titlechain is registered separately with a fully inlined prompt implementing DESIGN-LANGUAGE §16 (slide titles as one running sentence with leading connectives following problem→thesis→mechanism→proof→moat→ask).
All registrations use { silent: true } and the file sets window.CV_AI._canvasCapsSeeded = true at the end.
Header comment states the design intent: registering the move does NOT duplicate the prompt text because the generic run takes a prompt via params.
standouts: The catalogue-as-dispatch pattern: capability id == the move name, so the data array IS the generative surface description and the registry inspector projection.
deck.titlechain is the one capability that breaks the 'prompt single-sourced in the canvas' pattern by inlining its rule-prompt — deliberately, citing DESIGN-LANGUAGE §16 as the single rule source.
A typo/spacing note: two entries ('build.colors', 'image.studio.generate') have non-aligned column spacing but are otherwise consistent.
conventions: Follows the project's registry-projection pattern: moves are data, registered, queryable, executable via CV_AI.execute.
Generic-run-with-handed-in-prompt keeps each canvas's bespoke prompt single-sourced where its domain helpers live.
Sets a _canvasCapsSeeded marker flag like the seed file's _seeded marker.
deck.titlechain departs from the generic-run convention by carrying its own inline prompt (consciously, for a single-sourced rule).
concerns: deck.titlechain inlines a long prompt string while the file's whole premise is that prompts stay single-sourced in canvases — a localized inconsistency (the comment justifies it, but it is a second prompt-home pattern in the same file).
notes: This file is the registry of EVERY per-canvas Vi move as data — terse tuples completing canvas-supplied prompts — so the AI catalogue and the inspector show the app's full generative surface; deck.titlechain is the one move with an inlined rule-prompt.
questions: Are the surface keys (e.g. 'colors','icons','registry','inbox') guaranteed to match the surface ids the context resolvers and setActiveSurface use elsewhere?
Since runText returns the raw provider reply and 'the canvas keeps its own parse', where is each canvas's parse — is there a risk a move is registered here but no canvas actually invokes it?
purpose_vs_actual: Match — the header says it registers every per-canvas generative move into one catalogue without duplicating prompts, and the code does exactly that (with deck.titlechain as a noted exception that inlines a single-sourced rule).
apparent_themes: Catalogue-as-dispatch / interface as a projection of the registry
Single-sourcing prompts (don't duplicate prompt text on registration)
Full generative surface made queryable and inheritable
Loud load-order enforcement (throw if CV_AI absent)
intention_signals: Trying to make 'what Vi can do' fully described by one data catalogue covering every screen's moves, so the registry inspector and the AI execute path read one synchronized source — completing 'the interface is a projection into both' for every surface.
novelty: Expressing ~30 heterogeneous per-screen generative moves as uniform data tuples sharing one generic prompt-completing run, so adding a move is a registration, not code.
contribution: Contributes the full per-canvas capability catalogue (the breadth of Vi's generative moves keyed by surface/family) and the generic-run convention that registers a move without copying its prompt, plus the deck.titlechain narrative-title-rail move.
summary_for_embedding: IIFE registering ~30 per-canvas Vi generative moves (color/icon/voice/pattern/component/build/deck/widget/wizard/type/inbox/chat + 2 image + deck.titlechain) into CV_AI as data capabilities sharing a generic prompt-completing run.
intention_for_embedding: To make the app's entire generative surface a single queryable catalogue of capabilities the inspector and AI both read, without duplicating each canvas's prompt.
